DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT CIRCLEPOINT, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION STEINWERT, SCOTT ,AN INDIVIDUAL O'NEIL, JOHN HEARING SET FOR MAR-23-2011 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANTS CIRCLEPOINT, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, SCOTT STEINWERT AND JOHN O'NEIL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO COMPLY WITH C R C 2.112(4), TO PLEAD A FRAUDULENT TRANSFER TO A THIRD PARTY AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 5, TO PLEAD THE ELEMENTS OF THE ALLEGED FRAUD WITH THE REQUIRED PARTICULARITY AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 6, TO PLEAD OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF WORKER'S COMPENSATION FOR CAUSE OF ACTION 7 AND TO PLEAD THE DISCRIMINATORY CONDUCT OF WHICH HE COMPLAINED AND THE RESULTING ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 8, SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1, TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING STATUTORY BASIS FOR MR. GARDINER REQUESTING RELIEF IN LIGHT OF PARAGRAPH 1B IN THE RECITAL OF THE BUY AND SELL AGREEMENT; SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 2, 3, 4, 5 AND 8 AND AS TO CIRCLE POINT AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 6 AND 7, OTHERWISE OVERRULED AS TO DEFENDANT'S REMAINING ARGUMENTS. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS CIRCLEPOINT, SCOTT STEINWERT AND JOHN O'NEIL'S DEMURRER TO VERIFIED SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES.

APPEARANCES: Amanda Metcalf, Esq. 454-0945 for pltf & opposing party; Juan Araneda, Esq. of Meckler Bulger Tilson Marick & Pearson LLP 644-0914 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendants' demurrer to the second amended complaint is overruled as to the first cause of action for involuntary dissolution because plaintiff has alleged that the corporation is a close corporation; sustained without leave to amend as to the second and fourth causes of action for breach of contract and conversion as to the individuals because Judge Busch previously dismissed those claims and the individuals are not liable for corporate debts; overruled as to the fourth cause of action for conversion as to the corporation because it is sufficiently pled; overruled as to fifth cause of action labeled shareholder oppression because that is a mislabeled breach of fiduciary duty cause of action that is well-pleaded; sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action because plaintiff does not allege outrageous conduct; and sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action for wrongful termination cause of action because plaintiff does not allege facts showing how his termination violated public policy. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS CIRCLEPOINT, SCOTT STEINWERT AND JOHN O'NEIL'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF VERIFIED SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES.

APPEARANCES: Amanda Metcalf, Esq. 454-0945 for pltf & opposing party; Juan Araneda, Esq. of Meckler Bulger Tilson Marick & Pearson LLP 644-0914 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendants' motion to strike the second amended complaint is denied as to the fifth cause of action and moot as to the remaining issues due to the ruling on defendants' demurrer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Ntc Of Joinder And Joinder By Defts(1) Demurrer To The Second Amended Compliaint(2) Motion To Strike Portions Of The Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS MARY BEAN, PHYLLIS POTTER, SARAH LAYTON WALLACE, BEN STRUMWASSER, CRAIG FIXLER, MARK PISANO, AND LORI OGDEN MOORE'S NOTICE OF JOINDER IN DEFENDANTS CIRCLEPOINT, SCOTT STEINWERT AND JOHN O'NEIL'S (1) DEMURRER TO VERIFIED SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND (2) MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF SECOND AMENDED VERIFIED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES.

APPEARANCES: Amanda Metcalf, Esq. 454-0945 for pltf & opposing party; Juan Araneda, Esq. of Meckler Bulger Tilson Marick & Pearson LLP 644-0914 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendants' joinder in the demurrer and motion to strike are ruled on in the same way as the court has ruled on the demurrer and motion to strike. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-28-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. AMANDA METCALF, TEL. NO. 454-0945 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. JUAN C. ARANEDA OF LONG & LEVIT LLP APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTIES.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANTS CIRCLEPOINT, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, SCOTT STEINWERT AND JOHN O'NEIL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO COMPLY WITH C R C 2.112(4), TO PLEAD A FRAUDULENT TRANSFER TO A THIRD PARTY AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 5, TO PLEAD THE ELEMENTS OF THE ALLEGED FRAUD WITH THE REQUIRED PARTICULARITY AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 6, TO PLEAD OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF WORKER'S COMPENSATION FOR CAUSE OF ACTION 7 AND TO PLEAD THE DISCRIMINATORY CONDUCT OF WHICH HE COMPLAINED AND THE RESULTING ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 8, SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1, TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING STATUTORY BASIS FOR MR. GARDINER REQUESTING RELIEF IN LIGHT OF PARAGRAPH 1B IN THE RECITAL OF THE BUY AND SELL AGREEMENT; SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 2, 3, 4, 5 AND 8 AND AS TO CIRCLE POINT AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 6 AND 7, OTHERWISE OVERRULED AS TO DEFENDANT'S REMAINING ARGUMENTS. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
